title: `quiver:diff-pack` Command
objective: Ship `npx create-quiver diff-pack` that summarizes semantic changes in `docs/ai/*` between the working tree and a git ref (default `main`): files added/removed, front-matter fields changed, sections added/removed, line counts.
description: When context files drift it's easy to miss why. A raw `git diff` on Markdown is noisy. This command parses the files on both sides (via `git show` for the ref side) and emits a structured summary that highlights front-matter key changes, heading-level changes, and file additions/removals. It does not replace `git diff`; it summarizes what reviewers need to notice.
must: Read current `docs/ai/*` and compare with the same paths at `--since <ref>` (default `main`)
Use `child_process.spawn('git', ['show', '<ref>:<path>'], {shell:false})` to read ref-side files cross-platform
Detect added files, removed files, and modified files
Parse YAML front-matter on both sides and emit key-level diff (added/removed/changed keys)
Parse Markdown headings and emit section-level diff (added/removed/renamed)
Add a `docs/COMMANDS.md` row and update `README.md` npm scripts list
not_included: Replacing `git diff` (this is a summary, not a full diff)
acceptance: `npx create-quiver diff-pack --since main` prints added/removed/modified file counts for `docs/ai/*`
Changing a front-matter key surfaces in the summary as `Front-matter changed: purpose`
Adding a `## New Section` heading surfaces as `Section added: New Section`
Removing a file surfaces as `Removed: docs/ai/OLD.md`
assumptions: Git is available in PATH; command exits non-zero with a clear message if not
YAML front-matter parser tolerates minor formatting drift
Markdown heading detection by regex is sufficient for our files (all authored by this project)
